title: Clary sage and bergamot solid perfume
subtitle: A beeswax and jojoba solid perfume with clary sage and bergamot essential oils
excerpt: A solid perfume is an anhydrous, travel-friendly alternative to liquid fragrance: beeswax gives it a firm set that melts slightly on contact with warm skin, releasing the scent. Clary sage gives a herbal, floral base; bergamot adds brightness. The combined essential oil rate of 16% is at the higher end for a solid perfume, giving a noticeable but not overpowering scent. Yield is approximately 60 g.
sourceNotes: Solid perfume formula: beeswax at 25% of total weight gives a firm set that is not too hard or waxy to apply. Jojoba as the carrier: its wax ester structure mimics sebum and has minimal scent of its own, so it does not compete with the fragrance. EO rate in solid perfumes: 15 to 25% is typical; 16% used here. Clary sage EO (Salvia sclarea): herbal, slightly musky, floral. Bergamot EO (Citrus bergamia, cold-pressed peel): phototoxic on skin; apply solid perfume to pulse points not exposed to direct sunlight, or use FCF (furocoumarin-free) bergamot. Application to pulse points: wrists, inner elbows, neck base; warm skin helps diffuse the scent.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 60 g solid perfume (fills 1 salve tin)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One salve tin per 60 g batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Sets within 1 hour at room temperature. Ready to use once solid.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 24 months in a lidded tin in a cool, dark place. Jojoba has an exceptionally long shelf life; beeswax is stable indefinitely. Discard if the perfume develops a rancid or off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A formulation containing no water. Anhydrous preparations do not require a preservative. Solid perfumes, balms, and wax melts are all anhydrous; they have a long shelf life and are ideal for travel as they cannot spill.

Areas of the body where blood vessels run close to the skin surface, generating warmth. The wrists, inner elbows, and base of the neck are common pulse points for applying solid perfume; the warmth helps diffuse the fragrance into the air around you.

content.text: wax-based fragrance: beeswax gives it structure; jojoba oil carries the essential oils on the skin. The combined fragrance load is 16% of the total weight, which is at the higher end for a skin-application product but typical for a solid perfume where you apply only a small amount. Clary sage and bergamot are both mid-to-top notes that release quickly on contact with warm skin at
. Bergamot is phototoxic on skin: apply to pulse points covered by clothing, or use FCF (furocoumarin-free) bergamot for daylight wear.

items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade; 25% of total weight; gives the firm, portable set
58% of total weight; minimal scent; very long shelf life
10% of total weight; herbal, floral base
6.7% of total weight; phototoxic; apply to covered skin or use FCF bergamot

content.text: Place the beeswax pellets in a heatproof bowl over barely simmering water. Melt gently, stirring occasionally. Once fully melted, remove from the heat.
Add the jojoba oil to the melted beeswax and stir to combine. Allow to cool to around 50°C, then add the clary sage and bergamot essential oils. Stir well.
Pour into the salve tin immediately. The mixture sets quickly; work without delay. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for 1 hour until fully solid.

Warm the surface of the perfume with a fingertip for a few seconds, then smooth onto pulse points: inner wrists, inner elbows, base of the neck. A very small amount is enough. Do not apply bergamot-containing products to skin exposed to direct sunlight.

Store in the lidded tin at room temperature. Keeps 24 months. Jojoba is exceptionally stable; beeswax does not degrade. The solid perfume may soften in warm rooms; keep cool in summer. Discard if the perfume develops an off smell.
